subject: Mathematics
title: Logic Gates: AND, OR, NOT in Hardware
description: A short lesson to help you apply Logic Gates: AND, OR, NOT in Hardware.

hook.text: Inside your computer, billions of tiny switches flip between 0 and 1. Each switch is a logic gate.

buildup.text: An AND gate outputs 1 only when both inputs are 1. An OR gate outputs 1 when at least one input is 1.

discovery.text: Combining these simple gates builds adders, multipliers, memory, and entire processors.

twist.text: A single NAND gate (NOT-AND) is universal—any logic function can be built from NAND gates alone.

climax.text: Modern processors contain billions of gates. All of computation reduces to simple Boolean operations.

punchline.text: Billions of yes-or-no switches produce everything your computer does.

quiz.question: Why is the NAND gate called 'universal'?
options: Any logic function can be built using only NAND gates
